response.justification: Bogdan is a capable full-stack and polyglot engineer with genuine breadth across web, mobile, and some infrastructure work, but he falls critically short on the non-negotiable minimum qualifications for this role. The job requires 8 years of C++ experience; his resume shows roughly 6 months at National Instruments and a student thesis project using C++, with C++ absent from his skills list entirely. Additionally, he has no cloud compute platform experience, no formal technical leadership role managing cloud or DevOps engineers, and his 15+ year career is overwhelmingly oriented toward TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web product engineering rather than large-scale systems infrastructure.
strengths: Demonstrated breadth across multiple languages and paradigms (TypeScript, DLang, Swift, Node.js, Java) showing adaptability and a polyglot engineering mindset
Strong testing culture and software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, organized public workshops and dojos)
Experience with high-throughput micro-services at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second, showing exposure to performance-sensitive distributed systems
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI, one thesis project) against a hard minimum requirement of 8 years — this alone disqualifies the application
No cloud compute platform or infrastructure leadership experience; AWS CDK usage in a pricing team context does not meet the 5-year large-scale infrastructure bar
No formal technical lead or engineering management role; staff engineer titles are present but without evidence of owning cross-team technical strategy or mentoring cloud engineers
